records.ety: [Originally p. p. of OE. sacren to consecrate, F. sacrer, fr. L. sacrare, fr. sacer sacred, holy, cursed. Cf. Consecrate, Execrate, Saint, Sexton.]

defs: Set apart by solemn religious ceremony; especially, in a good sense, made holy; set apart to religious use; consecrated; not profane or common; as, a sacred place; a sacred day; sacred service.
Relating to religion, or to the services of religion; not secular; religious; as, sacred history.
quotes: Smit with the love of sacred song.

defs: Designated or exalted by a divine sanction; possessing the highest title to obedience, honor, reverence, or veneration; entitled to extreme reverence; venerable.
quotes: Such neighbor nearness to our sacred [royal] blood Should nothing privilege him.
Poet and saint to thee alone were given, The two most sacred names of earth and heaven.

defs: Hence, not to be profaned or violated; inviolable.
quotes: Secrets of marriage still are sacred held.
A temple, sacred to the queen of love.

defs: Solemnly devoted, in a bad sense, as to evil, vengeance, curse, or the like; accursed; baleful.
quotes: But, to destruction sacred and devote.
